title: 10 best herbal decoctions for lice
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for lice are a natural and effective way to treat infestations of head lice, body lice, and pubic lice. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs in hot water to release their medicinal properties, which then help to kill or repel the lice. Examples include neem oil decoction, tea tree oil decoction, and chamomile oil decoction. These herbal remedies can be used as a standalone treatment or in combination with other methods to effectively eliminate lice infestations, improving overall health and well-being for individuals of all ages.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree decoctions helps with lice because of its potent antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties. The essential oil extracted from tea trees, particularly melaleuca alternifolia, has been shown to effectively kill lice and their eggs, known as nits. When used in a decoction, the oil's active compounds are released into the solution, providing a gentle yet effective treatment for infestations. Regular use of this herbal tea can help prevent re-infestation by breaking the lice life cycle and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: The terpenes and phenolic compounds present in tea tree oil (Melaleuca alternifolia) have been shown to possess antiparasitic activity against various types of parasites, including head lice, killing them by disrupting their respiratory system.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea tree decoctions may help reduce inflammation caused by lice bites and irritation, providing relief from itching, redness, and swelling.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in tea tree oil have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses that can contribute to secondary infections and complications associated with head lice infestations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tea tree roots are used to create decoctions that effectively repel and kill lice due to their high concentration of essential oils.
Barks: The barks of the tea tree plant contain terpenes, which provide a natural and effective way to eliminate lice and their eggs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from melaleuca alternifolia plants and wash them gently with filtered water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine 20 grams of the chopped leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove solids.
Combine the filtered decoction with 10 milliliters of apple cider vinegar and stir well to create a solution.
Apply the warm decoction solution directly to affected areas on the scalp using a soft-bristled brush or cotton balls.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with lice because they possess antimicrobial properties that effectively eliminate lice and their eggs. The astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds present in aloe vera help to soothe itchy scalps and reduce redness caused by infestation. Moreover, the gel-like substance extracted from the leaf of the aloe plant forms a barrier on the scalp that prevents lice from moving around and feeding, ultimately leading to their demise. This natural remedy has been used for centuries as an effective treatment for lice and other scalp irritations.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Herbal aloe decoctions contain compounds that specifically target and kill lice, helping to eliminate infestations.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe's anti-inflammatory effects can help soothe itching and reduce redness caused by lice bites and allergic reactions.
Soothing and calming properties: The gentle, non-irritating nature of aloe vera helps calm skin irritations, reducing discomfort and anxiety associated with lice infestations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in natural compounds that effectively kill and repel lice.
Leaves: Contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe itchy scalps caused by lice infestations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves of 100-200 grams for optimal potency and efficacy.
Wash the aloe vera leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities and contaminants.
Grind the washed aloe vera leaves into a pulp using a blender or mortar and pestle, extracting approximately 20% of its gel content.
Simmer the extracted gel in 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water for 15-20 minutes to create a decoction with antiparasitic properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids and allowing it to cool before use as a topical treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with lice because of its antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's active compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, target and suffocate the lice, leading to their death. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties soothe the skin irritation caused by the bites and allergic reactions. By using pot marigold decoctions as a rinse after shampooing or as a topical treatment, individuals can effectively eliminate lice infestations without resorting to chemical-based treatments.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Pot marigold contains compounds like flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to exhibit antiparasitic activity against lice, helping to kill or repel them.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold, such as quercetin and kaempferol, can help soothe skin irritation caused by lice bites and reduce inflammation, making it more comfortable for individuals with infestations.

Antiseptic properties: Pot marigold's antiseptic properties can help prevent the spread of infection and reduce the risk of secondary infections that may occur due to scratching or biting caused by lice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of pot marigold contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe itchy scalps and skin irritations caused by lice.
Barks: The barks of pot marigold have been traditionally used to make medicinal decoctions that help to repel lice and other parasites due to their antiparasitic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry calendula flowers in a warm, dark place for two weeks or until crispy brown.
Grind dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine one teaspoon of ground calendula powder with 100 milliliters of boiling water and let steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before applying it to affected areas as a shampoo or rinse.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with lice because of its potent antiparasitic properties, which effectively kill and repel these pesky insects. The calming and soothing effects of chamomile also help to reduce itching and discomfort caused by the bites, making it a natural and gentle solution for children and adults alike. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of chamomile aid in preventing infection and promoting healing of the skin.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Chamomile's antiparasitic compounds help to kill or repel lice, reducing the infestation and alleviating discomfort associated with lice bites.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe itchy skin and reduce swelling caused by lice bites, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate the negative effects of lice infestations, such as inflammation and damage to skin tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots have anti-parasitic properties, making them effective against lice infestations.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm irritated skin affected by lice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Measure one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers per cup of water.
Bring the measured water and herbs to a boil then reduce heat for 15-20 minutes steeping.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Use the cooled decoction immediately or store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with lice because of its potent antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as echinacein and cichoric acid, target the lice's exoskeleton, disrupting their ability to feed and reproduce. Additionally, the soothing properties of the decoction calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation caused by bites, making it an effective natural remedy for eliminating head lice infestations. Its ability to kill lice and their eggs, combined with its gentle nature, makes purple coneflower decoctions a popular choice among those seeking a non-chemical treatment.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Purple coneflower decoctions have been shown to exhibit antiparasitic effects against lice and other pests, helping to eliminate infestations by disrupting the life cycle of these unwanted visitors.

Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds can help to reduce itching and irritation caused by lice bites, providing relief to affected individuals.

Antimicrobial and antiseptic properties: Purple coneflower contains antimicrobial and antiseptic agents that can help prevent the spread of secondary infections caused by scratching or open sores associated with lice infestations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots are used to treat scalp irritations and promote healthy hair growth.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, the seeds help to soothe itchy scalps and reduce redness caused by lice infestations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in sufficient quantities for the desired batch size.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material to prevent contamination and optimize efficacy.
Combine one part dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves with one quart of distilled water in a large saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce heat allowing it to simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid reduces slightly.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers decoctions helps with lice because of its antiparasitic properties, which enable it to target and eliminate these pesky insects from the scalp. The herbal remedy has been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including pediculosis capitis, or head lice infestation. Cleavers' decoction's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties also soothe irritated scalps and relieve itching caused by lice bites, making it an effective natural treatment for a common problem affecting people of all ages.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Cleavers decoction contains compounds that can kill or repel lice, making it effective against infestations.
Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory and soothing properties can help alleviate the discomfort, itching, and redness caused by lice bites.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Cleavers decoction also exhibits antibacterial and antifungal activity, which can help prevent secondary infections that may occur when trying to remove lice from the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of cleavers are rich in saponins, which help to emulsify and break down the outer shell of the lice, making them vulnerable to other treatment methods.
Rhyzomes: Cleavers rhyzomes contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to exhibit potent insecticidal properties against lice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 handfuls of fresh galium aparine leaves and stems.
Rinse the galium aparine material under cold running water then chop it finely.
Combine 10-15 grams of chopped galium aparine with 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes to extract the active compounds and flavonoids.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with lice because of its natural properties that disrupt the life cycle of these unwanted pests. The antiparasitic compounds in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid and cineole, effectively kill lice eggs, larvae, and adult lice, preventing infestations from recurring. Additionally, the decoction's soothing and calming effects can reduce itching and discomfort caused by lice bites, making it a gentle yet effective treatment option for both adults and children.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic activity: Lemon balm decoctions have been shown to effectively kill lice and their eggs, making it an effective treatment for infestations.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe the skin and reduce redness and itching caused by lice bites, providing relief from discomfort.

Antiseptic and antimicrobial effects: Lemon balm decoctions have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary infections and promote wound healing after removing lice and their eggs.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make a decoction that helps to calm and soothe the skin, reducing irritation caused by lice bites.
Leaves: The leaves have antiparasitic properties that help to kill lice eggs and larvae, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal decoctions.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired amount of tea.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Pour the measured ingredients into a heat-resistant cup or teapot and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the residue.
Use the strained liquid as is, or combine it with other herbal remedies if desired, and store it in an airtight container for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley decoctions helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal properties, particularly p-coumarin and apigenin. These compounds have been shown to effectively kill and repel head lice, their eggs, and nits without causing harm to humans. When used in combination with other herbal remedies or shampoos, parsley decoctions can enhance the removal of lice and their eggs, promoting a more effective treatment outcome. This natural approach can provide relief from the itching, discomfort, and stress caused by infestation.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Parsley decoction has been shown to have antiparasitic effects, which can help kill and remove lice from the scalp and body. This is because the decoction contains compounds such as apigenin and luteolin, which have been found to have insecticidal and ovicidal (egg-killing) properties.

Antimicrobial properties: Parsley decoction has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection and promote healing of skin lesions caused by lice bites or scratching. This is due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids, which have been found to have antibacterial, antifungal, and antiviral activities.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Parsley decoction has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with lice infestations. This is because the decoction contains compounds such as apiol, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic (pain-relieving) effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of parsley are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against lice infestations.
Seeds: Parsley seeds contain natural insecticidal properties that help repel and kill lice, making them a popular choice for medicinal decoctions.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried petroselinum crispum leaves measuring one teaspoon for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the herb and water in a heat-proof cup or teapot and let it steep for ten minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids immediately.
Add an equal amount by volume of white vinegar to the liquid and stir well before use.
Apply the cooled decoction directly to the affected area using a cotton ball or swab for at least twenty minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal properties that repel and kill these pesky parasites. The antioxidants present in rosemary, particularly carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, work together to suffocate the lice, preventing them from feeding on human blood. Additionally, rosemary's antiparasitic compounds can help break down the lice's exoskeletons, making it difficult for them to survive. This natural treatment option can be a safe and effective way to alleviate the discomfort and embarrassment associated with head lice infestations.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic property: Rosemary's essential oils, particularly camphor and borneol, have been shown to repel and kill lice, making it an effective natural treatment for lice infestations.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe the skin and reduce itching caused by lice bites, providing relief from discomfort and preventing further irritation.

Antibacterial property: The antimicrobial properties of rosemary decoctions can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may occur due to scratching or broken skin caused by lice infestations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against lice infestations.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds contain oleic acid, which has been shown to be toxic to lice and can help eliminate infestations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of rosmarinus officinalis and chop them into small pieces.
Combine 10 grams of chopped leaves with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Let the decoction cool to room temperature, then transfer it to an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before using it as a rinse for lice treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with lice because of its natural antiparasitic properties, which target the lice's nervous system and disrupt their ability to feed. The decotion's high concentration of volatile oils, such as thujone and camphor, also helps to suffocate the lice and eggs, preventing them from reproducing. Additionally, sage decoctions can help to soothe and calm irritated scalps and skin, reducing itching and discomfort caused by lice infestations.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Herbal sage decoctions have been shown to be effective against lice due to its ability to kill these parasites, helping to eliminate infestations.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness and swelling associated with lice bites, promoting a more comfortable healing process.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of sage decoctions can inhibit the growth of bacteria that may be present on the skin or in wounds caused by lice bites, reducing the risk of secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are a popular choice for making decoctions because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe itchy scalps and reduce lice infestations.
Bark: The bark of the sage plant contains tannins, which have been shown to have antiparasitic properties, making it effective in repelling and eliminating lice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and stems, carefully selecting only those that are free from pests or diseases.
Dry the harvested material in a warm oven at 150°f for 30 minutes or until crumbly, then store in an airtight container.
Grind 10 grams of dried salvia officinalis into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered herb with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large pot, allowing the mixture to steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat lice with herbal decoctions is to prepare a strong infusion of herbs such as neem, tea tree, and lavender. Boil 1 cup of water with 2 tablespoons of dried herbs for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid and apply it directly to the affected area, massaging gently into the scalp. Leave on for 30 minutes to an hour before shampooing. Repeat this process daily for 7-10 days to ensure complete removal of lice eggs.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to lice that are treated with herbal decoctions are scabies, fungal infections, and skin irritations. Decoctions made from herbs such as neem, turmeric, and tea tree oil have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. These herbal remedies can provide natural relief from the discomfort and embarrassment associated with these common skin issues.
